name: Bahnar

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Bahnar (bahn1262).

notes: Austroasiatic (Bahnaric) language of the Vietnamese Central Highlands, written in a Vietnamese-modeled Latin missionary orthography. The map covers the Vietnamese-style consonant digraphs (nh, ng/ngh, ch, kh, ph, th) and quality-bearing vowel letters (a, ă, â, e, ê, o, ô, ơ, u, ư); b and d are read as the implosives characteristic of the inventory. Bahnar is atonal, so no tone is at stake, but phonemic vowel length is only inconsistently written and is left unencoded, and the orthography is not fully standardized across missionary traditions, so spelling variants and the exact reading of b/d (implosive vs plain) raise the expected error floor. Onset clusters (bl, kr, hm, hng, etc.) are read as concatenations of their members.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Bahnar (bahn1262)
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for Evolutionary Anthropology
